description: §41.16 — `pick=["X", ...]` on `<tableFor for=T>` naming a field not present on struct `T` fires E-TABLEFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD. `User` has `{ id, name }`; `pick=["nope"]` names a non-field. Mirror of E-FORMFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD (§41.14) + E-SCHEMAFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD (§41.15). Complements the `tablefor-column-field-unknown` case (an unknown `<column field>`) by exercising the `pick=` attribute path specifically. (Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 + W-STDLIB-SEED-FAILCLOSED co-fire, as in the sibling table-for cases; superset match.)
rationale: §41.16 E-TABLEFOR-PICK-INVALID-FIELD: the `pick=[...]` attribute SHALL name only declared struct fields; an unknown name is rejected. Fix: correct or remove the invalid field name from the pick array.
